background: Exercise 18.3 found that 43% of CMV+ patients experienced arterial restenosis within 6 months of atherectomy. In contrast, 8% of CMV-patients experienced a similar outcome. Table 18.8 contains observed counts.

task.query: Use a chi-square statistic to test the association for statistical significance.
task.answer:
$X_{\text {stat }}^2=9.879, d f=1, P=0.0017$

With continuity-correction, $X^2$ stat, $c=8.294, d f=1, P=0.0040$

task.query: Repeat the test with a $z$-statistic ($\$ 17.3). Show the relation between the chi-square statistic and the $z$-statistic.
task.answer:
$z_{\text {stat }}=3.14, P=0.0017$. Note that $z^2_{\text {stat }}=3.14^2=9.86 \approx X^2$ stat

With continuity-correction, $z^2$ stat., $c=2.88^2=8.30 \approx X^2$ stat, c

data:

\hline & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Restenosed } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Did not restenose } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Total }\
\hline
\hline CMV + & 21 & 28 & 49 \
\hline CMV- & 2 & 24 & 26 \
\hline Total & 23 & 49 & 75 \
